description:
A SINGLE UNINTERRUPTED PANORAMIC PAINTING of one long combined STORAGE-AND-WORKSHOP interior (entrepôt + atelier d'artisan) sharing the same continuous building under one roof, rendered as ONE flowing seamless mural with absolutely NO vertical lines dividing the image, NO panels, NO image seams, NO black borders — strictly forbidden to split the image visually into sections; the result must read as a single oil-painted long view of the same hangar-like room.

ABSOLUTELY CRITICAL compositional constraint: this panorama will be viewed on a smartphone that swipes horizontally and snaps to FOUR viewing windows (0–25%, 25–50%, 50–75%, 75–100% of the width). For clean swipes, NO furniture, NO rack, NO window, NO shelving unit, NO architectural feature MAY CROSS OR STRADDLE the vertical positions at exactly 25%, 50%, or 75%. Every major object must be contained ENTIRELY within ONE of the four quarters. The areas around 25%, 50%, and 75% (roughly 23–27%, 48–52%, 73–77%) must contain ONLY plain continuous back wall and worn floor — these are the 'breathing zones' for swipe boundaries.

Architecture: the viewer looks into a long warm artisan's storage-and-workshop building from straight-on, like a cross-section view at eye-level. The BACK WALL runs unbroken across the full width (from 0% to 100%), painted warm aged plaster with exposed vertical wooden framing posts and timber studs visible here and there. A LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward at the far left (0–8%), a RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward at the far right (92–100%). ONE continuous floor of WORN, DUSTY wide wooden planks runs unbroken across the bottom — uneven, slightly stained, with patches of fine dust at the foot of every piece of furniture and faint sawdust accents in the workshop half. ONE continuous exposed dark-wood ceiling beam/truss runs unbroken across the top. The ceiling is high (the building is a single tall space ≈4–5m high), allowing high windows on the back wall in the workshop half.

Feature details by horizontal position (0% = left edge, 100% = right edge):

• 0–8% — LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward, plain warm plaster, one exposed wooden timber post, empty floor.

• 7–22% — SECTION 1 (STORAGE LEFT): a tall INDUSTRIAL STORAGE RACK (rayonnage métallique) placed PERPENDICULAR to the back wall — its long horizontal axis runs from the back wall toward the viewer, so we see the rack from a slight 3/4 SIDE view. The rack's rear short end is set against the back wall around 13–20% horizontal; its front short end faces the viewer around 8–11% horizontal (slightly larger / lower in the image due to perspective). Industrial vintage construction: dark forest-green painted STEEL ANGLE-IRON UPRIGHTS (riveted), with FIVE empty horizontal metal shelves stacked at evenly spaced heights from near the floor up to about 1.9m. All shelves are COMPLETELY EMPTY — naked patinated metal, ready to receive composited items. Faint surface patina, fine dust on shelves and on the floor at the base of the rack. Fully contained within Section 1 (right edge of rack ≤22%, leaving ≥1% margin before the 23% start of the breathing zone).

• 23–27% — BREATHING ZONE: ONLY plain continuous back wall + worn dusty floor, no rack, no feature. The 25% mark must be perfectly empty.

• 28–47% — SECTION 2 (STORAGE RIGHT): a SECOND tall INDUSTRIAL STORAGE RACK, identical construction, dimensions, and orientation to the first one, also PERPENDICULAR to the back wall. Its rear short end against the back wall around 33–42% horizontal; its front short end toward the viewer around 28–32% horizontal. Same dark forest-green steel angle-iron, five empty horizontal shelves, dust at base. Fully contained within Section 2 (right edge ≤47%, left edge ≥28%). Shelves COMPLETELY EMPTY.

• 48–52% — BREATHING ZONE: ONLY plain back wall + worn dusty floor. The 50% mark must be perfectly empty. This is also the natural visual transition between the STORAGE half (left) and the WORKSHOP half (right) — same building, but a subtle shift: the back wall here may show very faint differences in plaster tone (slightly cleaner in the workshop half), and the ceiling rises just a touch on the right. Just enough hint that the viewer reads 'storage ends, workshop begins' without breaking the architecture or adding any explicit divider.

• 53–72% — SECTION 3 (WORKSHOP LEFT): on the BACK WALL, a PROFESSIONAL ARTISAN WORKBENCH (établi de menuisier) centered around 62% horizontal, fully contained within Section 3 (left edge ≥55%, right edge ≤71%). Real workshop workbench: ~1.5m wide, ~85–90cm tall (top at hip-level for a standing adult), THICK worn-beech/oak top (8–10cm thick slab), four heavy square legs with sturdy lower stretchers, and a CAST-IRON BENCH VICE (étau) bolted to the front-left corner of the top with visible turning handle. The top is COMPLETELY EMPTY. HIGH ON THE BACK WALL above the workbench (well above the bench top), a HIGH WORKSHOP WINDOW (fenêtre d'atelier haute) fully contained within Section 3 (left edge ≥55%, right edge ≤71%): a wide rectangular wooden-framed window with the sill at about head-height (~2m above the floor), with 4 vertical wooden mullions dividing it into clean utilitarian panes (no Art Nouveau imposte, no decoration — these are functional workshop windows). Through the panes we see SKY with soft clouds and the upper foliage of nearby trees, warm afternoon daylight streaming in and casting soft pools of light onto the workbench top below.

• 73–77% — BREATHING ZONE: ONLY plain continuous back wall + worn dusty floor. The 75% mark must be perfectly empty.

• 78–95% — SECTION 4 (WORKSHOP RIGHT): on the BACK WALL, a SMALLER ARTISAN WORKBENCH (petit établi) centered around 83% horizontal, fully contained within Section 4 (left edge ≥78%, right edge ≤88%). Same real workshop construction as the central workbench but narrower: ~0.85m wide, same ~85–90cm working height, thick worn-wood top, sturdy square legs with lower stretchers. Top is COMPLETELY EMPTY. HIGH ON THE BACK WALL above this small workbench, a SECOND HIGH WORKSHOP WINDOW (smaller, fully contained within Section 4, centered around 83%, left edge ≥78%, right edge ≤88%): rectangular wooden frame with 2 vertical mullions, sill at the same head-height as the other workshop window. Through the panes we see the same SKY + tree-top view, same warm light. To the RIGHT of this small workbench, against the back wall and partly onto the angled right side wall, a TALL EMPTY METAL SHELVING UNIT (étagère métallique industrielle) centered around 91% horizontal, fully contained within Section 4 (left edge ≥89%, right edge ≤95%). Same industrial vintage construction as the storage racks (dark forest-green painted steel angle-iron, riveted), with FOUR HORIZONTAL EMPTY metal shelves, 1.8m tall, COMPLETELY EMPTY.

• 95–100% — RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward, plain plaster, one exposed vertical wooden timber post, empty.

Floor across the entire panorama: WORN, DUSTY wide wooden planks running horizontally. Patina, faint stains, fine dust accumulating at the foot of every piece of furniture, subtle sawdust hints in the workshop half (right). No furniture or stray objects on the floor outside the listed pieces.

Lighting: soft warm directional light from the upper-left, plus the two high workshop windows on the back wall (right half) acting as bright daylight sources. The storage half (left) is noticeably dimmer than the workshop half, with daylight filtering across from the workshop side and dust visible in soft slanted light beams. Consistent across the whole panorama. Cohesive Art Déco rendering: cream parchment paper texture, sepia ink line-art, soft forest green wash, brass and warm wood tones, subtle paper grain. No people, no text, no captions, no signatures, no watermark.

CRITICAL FINAL CONSTRAINTS:
(a) ABSOLUTELY NO furniture, rack, workbench, window, or shelving unit crosses or straddles the vertical positions at 25%, 50%, or 75% — those positions are strictly plain back wall + worn dusty floor.
(b) The two storage racks (Sections 1 and 2) are PERPENDICULAR to the back wall: their long axis runs from the back wall forward toward the viewer, seen at a slight 3/4 side view. Their short ends face the viewer (front, larger) and the back wall (rear, smaller via perspective).
(c) The two windows (Sections 3 and 4) are HIGH WORKSHOP WINDOWS on the back wall (sill at ~2m, well above the workbench tops), utilitarian wooden frames with straight vertical mullions, NO Art Nouveau decoration, NO imposte.
(d) Workbenches are REAL workshop workbenches (NOT desks, NOT tables, NOT coffee tables): thick wooden top at standard working height (~85–90cm = hip-level for a standing adult), sturdy heavy square legs with lower stretchers, the central one having a bolted-on cast-iron bench vice.
(e) All racks, shelves, workbenches are COMPLETELY EMPTY — bare metal and bare wood, ready to receive composited items later.
(f) Storage racks AND end-of-Section-4 shelves are ALL METAL (steel angle-iron, industrial vintage, dark forest-green), NOT wood.
(g) Floor is WORN and DUSTY throughout — wide wooden planks, fine dust at the base of furniture, faint sawdust in the workshop half.
(h) No transparent areas — PNG is fully opaque. No vertical seams, no panel borders, no black bars.
